definition: a classic is a book, film, or piece of music that is considered to be of very high quality and that people think will be admired for a long time into the future
example: have you read The Hunger Games? It’s a classic
The Catcher in the Rye is a classic American novel

definition: a costume is the set of clothes that an actor wears when he or she is acting on the stage or in a film
example: he was back on stage in the third act after another costume change

definition: an exhibition is an event where people can come and see items such as paintings, sculptures etc that are brought together and displayed in one place
example: the exhibition will run from  April 14th to August 30th
the museum exhibits aircraft, cars, and industrial machinery

definition: if something is halfway along something, or halfway through it, it is around the middle of it
example: the shop is halfway down Oxford Street
the leading runners have just reached the halfway stage in the London marathon

definition: historical plays, films, stories, etc are about people or events in the past. Historical people or events existed in the past
example: he wrote a book on Shakespeare’s historical plays

definition: an installation is a piece of art where objects, lights or pictures are placed in a space to make people think about something
example: her current project is an installation for the Ikon Gallery in Birmingham

definition: the landscape is the view that you can see when you are in the country, away from towns and buildings. A landscape is also a painting or photograph of the countryside
example: a famous landscape artist

definition: in a theatre or on a film set, the lighting is the system of different lights that can be operated during a performance to create different effects
example: a lighting technician

definition: an object is a physical thing that you can touch and feel, and that is not alive
example: she could see an object floating in the middle of the river

definition: the plot of a story, film, or play is all the linked events that happen during it
example: the plot was very complicated

definition: a portrait is a painting, photograph, or drawing of someone which is meant to show what they look like
example: Andrew Wyeth was a leading American portrait painter

definition: at a cinema, theatre, or concert hall, the programme is the whole collection of events that you see at one time. A programme is also a printed document that tells you about the performance that you are going to see
example: the programme starts at 7

definition: if you queue, or queue up, you wait in a line of people until it is your turn, for example to buy something or go in somewhere
example: we queued for two hours to get tickets
we were asked to form a queue outside the theatre

definition: something that is rural is in the countryside or connected to the countryside
example: they live in a small rural community

definition: steps are the flat narrow surfaces that make up a staircase and that you tread on as you go up or down
example: shall we meet on the steps outside the theatre?

definition: subtitles are the words of a film or TV programme which are written on the screen
example: a Brazilian film with English subtitles
the film’s in Russian, but don’t worry, it’s subtitled

definition: a tragedy is a very sad and shocking event in which people suffer badly or die. In literature, a tragedy is a story in which the main character cannot control events and is killed
example: I’d rather see a comedy than a tragedy
Hamlet is probably Shakespeare’s most famous tragic hero
tragically, he died before his book was published

definition: your heart beats all the time you are alive. When it beats, it gets slightly bigger then goes back to its earlier size as it pumps blood around your body. If you say that the heart of a place is beating, you mean it is very lively there
example: the city’s heart is still beating
put your hand here. Can you feel the beat of my heart?

definition: if something is booming, the amount of activity is increasing and it is very successful
example: the economy has boomed since the discovery of oil
there’s been a huge boom in exports

definition: when people cheer, they make a lot of noise to show that they are happy and like something that they have just seen or heard
example: all the fans were cheering and singing
a tremendous cheer went up at the end of the game

definition: if something or someone converts you, they make you feel enthusiastic about something
example: one trip to a game was enough to convert me to baseball
my conversion from motorist to cyclist happened last summer

definition: if you disturb someone, you interrupt them in an annoying or unpleasant way
example: the police are unlikely to disturb you
we’ll try not to cause too much disturbance during the building work

definition: if something or someone fools you, they make you believe something that is not true
example: you can’t fool me
collocations: be fooled by someone or something

definition: if you get away with a crime or with doing something wrong, you are not caught and punished for it
example: it’s too risky, you’ll never get away with it

definition: if you head somewhere, you go there or go towards there
example: I’m going to head for Manchester and try to get there by 10

definition: if you are overdressed, you are wearing clothes that are more formal or smarter than they need to be, and all the other people around you are dressed more casually
example: I thought I’d be overdressed in a suit, but luckily I’m not the only one

definition: to restrict an activity means to limit it or control it by having rules or laws about it
example: the new laws restrict freedom of the press
the government has introduced tight restrictions on immigration

definition: when the sun sets, it gets very low in the sky and finally disappears from sight at the end of the day
example: the sun doesn’t set until 11 at night here
the sun had risen an hour before I got up

definition: supplies are things you need, such as food, water, soap and other everyday things
example: you can buy supplies there till after midnight

definition: if something goes upwards, it goes towards a higher place. If a number is upwards of another number, it is higher than that number
example: even a simple burger costs upwards of $20

definition: if you break a rule, you do not do what you should do. If you break a promise, you do not do what you promised you would do
example: I’ll never forgive you if you break this promise
he never paid me back – yet another broken promise

definition: the council is the local government in an area
example: the local council is responsible for the schools
she has been a local councillor for the last twenty years

definition: if you exaggerate, you say that something is bigger, better, or more extreme than it really is
example: maybe I’m exaggerating. It’s not the worst club, but it was pretty bad
OK, I admit, that was a slight exaggeration

definition: if you get a lift somewhere, someone takes you there in their car
example: luckily, she gave me a lift to the station

definition: if something is moving, it makes you feel sad or emotional
example: a really moving story
his speech really moved me

definition: if you feel out of place, you feel nervous or uncomfortable because the place or situation you are in does not suit you, or you feel that you do not belong there
example: everyone else was so much older, I felt really out of place
translation: не на своем месте, не в своей тарелке

definition: if you think something is overrated, you think it is not as good as many people say it is
example: I think the film was hugely overrated

definition: if you make a prediction, you say what you think will happen in the future
example: his predictions never come true
can history help us predict the future?
the ending of the film was quite predictable
the results were, predictably, not good
the ending of the film was completely unpredictable

definition: if something is sold out, you cannot buy any of it because people have already bought all of it. If a show or concert is sold out, people have bought all the tickets
example: we couldn’t get tickets because the show was completely  sold out
the concert will sell out very quickly

definition: if you sweat, water comes out through your skin, for example because you are very hot after exercise or because you are ill
example: running for the bus made me sweat
sweat was running down his back
he came back from his run hot and sweaty

definition: tears are small drops of liquid which come from your eyes when you cry. If someone is in tears, they are crying because they are upset or because something is sad
example: I was in tears by the end of the film

definition: something that is weird is very strange
example: it was a great exhibition, but the paintings were a bit weird
she was wearing a rather weirdly shaped coat

definition: an arranged marriage is one in which the two people are told to marry each other by their parents, instead of choosing freely who they want to marry
example: she ran away from home to avoid an arranged marriage

definition: cattle are cows and bulls that are kept on a farm and used for meat and milk production
example: most of the Dorobo people raise cattle and farm crops

definition: if you raise animals, you keep them and look after them from when they are born until they are ready to be taken away to be killed for food. If you raise plants, you grow them and then sell them for food
example: most of the Dorobo people raise cattle and farm crops

definition: if you regret something you have done, you feel bad about it and wish you hadn’t done it
example: I deeply regret what happened that day in Rome
he has finally expressed regret for his unacceptable behaviour
